Class InputHandler

java.lang.Object
com.comp2042.controller.input.InputHandler
public class InputHandler extends Object
Handles all keyboard input for the game, processing key events for both single player and multiplayer modes. This class separates input handling logic from the GUI controller, managing key bindings, soft drop rates, hard drop processing, and coordinating with game callbacks to execute player actions. It supports key rebinding through the settings panel and handles special states such as pause, game over, and ready states in multiplayer.

  • Constructor Details

    • InputHandler

      public InputHandler(KeyBindingsManager keyBindingsManager)
      Creates an InputHandler with required dependencies. Uses constructor injection to follow Dependency Inversion Principle.
      Parameters:
      keyBindingsManager - The key bindings manager for resolving key mappings

  • Method Details

    • setSettingsPanel

      public void setSettingsPanel(SettingsPanel settingsPanel)
      Sets the settings panel reference for handling key rebinding operations.
      Parameters:
      settingsPanel - The settings panel instance that manages key rebinding UI

    • setSinglePlayerCallbacks

      public void setSinglePlayerCallbacks(InputHandler.SinglePlayerCallbacks callbacks)
      Sets the callback interface for single player game actions. These callbacks are used to execute game actions when keys are pressed in single player mode.
      Parameters:
      callbacks - The callback interface implementation for single player actions

    • setMultiplayerCallbacks

      public void setMultiplayerCallbacks(InputHandler.MultiplayerCallbacks callbacks)
      Sets the callback interface for multiplayer game actions. These callbacks are used to execute game actions when keys are pressed in multiplayer mode.
      Parameters:
      callbacks - The callback interface implementation for multiplayer actions

    • handleKeyPress

      public void handleKeyPress(javafx.scene.input.KeyEvent keyEvent, boolean isMultiplayerMode, boolean gameStarted)
      Handles key press events for both single and multiplayer modes. Processes keyboard input to execute game actions such as movement, rotation, drops, and hold operations. Also handles special keys like pause, new game, and ready states. If the settings panel is visible and rebinding is active, the key event is forwarded to the settings panel for key rebinding instead of being processed as a game action.
      Parameters:
      keyEvent - The key event that was pressed
      isMultiplayerMode - True if the game is in multiplayer mode, false for single player
      gameStarted - True if the game has started, false if it's in the initial/ready state

    • handleKeyRelease

      public void handleKeyRelease(javafx.scene.input.KeyEvent keyEvent, boolean isMultiplayerMode)
      Handles key release events, primarily for resetting soft drop rate when the soft drop key is released. When the soft drop key is released, the game timeline rate is restored to normal speed for the appropriate player.
      Parameters:
      keyEvent - The key event that was released
      isMultiplayerMode - True if the game is in multiplayer mode, false for single player
